{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Multiprocessing refactor introduces potential resource leaks and race conditions around shared state management across multiple processes.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,
    "count": 0,

    "details": ""
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Resource Management",
      "file": "src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py",
      "line": 73,
      "title": "SpansBuffer created per process without cleanup mechanism",
      "description": "New `SpansBuffer` instances are created for each process in `_create_process_for_shards()` and stored in `self.buffers` dict, but there is no corresponding cleanup/teardown logic when processes are restarted or the flusher is stopped. This can lead to resource leaks (Redis connections, memory) as buffers accumulate without being properly closed.",
      "suggestion": "Add a cleanup method that closes/releases all buffers in `self.buffers` before creating new ones. Call this during process restart and in a `__del__` or `join()` method."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "Concurrency",
      "file": "src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py",
      "line": 47,
      "title": "Race condition: max_processes depends on uninitialized buffer.assigned_shards",
      "description": "Line 47 uses `len(buffer.assigned_shards)` to set `self.max_processes`, but `buffer` is never stored as `self.buffer`. Later, the buffer is created per-process (line 73), making it unclear if `assigned_shards` is available before process creation or if processes have different shard assignments. This creates a mismatch between the initial shard calculation and actual shard assignment per process.",
      "suggestion": "Store the original buffer as `self.buffer` and ensure `assigned_shards` is consistent before process creation, or document why per-process buffers may have different shard assignments than the parent."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "Logic Error",
      "file": "src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.py",
      "line": 131,
      "title": "Incomplete method definition: _create_process_for_shard",
      "description": "The method `_create_process_for_shard` (starting at line 131) is incomplete—the diff shows it truncated mid-implementation with only a docstring and incomplete logic (`for process_index, shards in self.process_to_shards_map.items(): ...` with no body). This will cause a syntax error or unexpected behavior.",
      "suggestion": "Complete the implementation of `_create_process_for_shard` or remove it if not yet needed."
    }
  ]
}
